title: Creating Custom Types 🏗️
description: Stop relying on basic Strings and Ints. Build your own complex data!

slides.title: Beyond Basic Types 🚀
slides.content:
# The Need for More

Until now, we've used basic types provided by Apple: `String`, `Int`, `Bool`, `Double`.

But what if you are making a game? How do you store a "Player"? A Player isn't just a String. A Player has a Name (String), a Score (Int), and isAlive (Bool).

We need to bundle all these different types together into a brand new, custom Type. We do this using **Structs**!

quizData.question: What is the primary purpose of a 'Struct' in Swift?

options.text: To bundle multiple related variables into a single, custom Data Type.

quizData.explanation: Structs allow developers to mold raw data (Strings, Ints) into complex, real-world objects (like a Player, a Car, or a Button).

slides.title: The Blueprint 🏗️

quizData.question: By convention in Swift, should the name of a Struct start with an uppercase or lowercase letter?
quizData.explanation: Because a Struct creates a brand new Type (just like String or Int), we always capitalize its name to distinguish it from normal variables.
